spec_match.rationale: Demo exercises the core API surface — every title keyword (custom, descriptors, webassembly) and 8 summary keywords appear directly in the page.

interactive.rationale: There is a try-it button and some script logic, but the interactive surface is minimal (1 form controls) — adding a live readout or more parameters would push it past the bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb and lede only share 2 keywords — page substance is close but the framing has drifted from how the feature index sells it.

edge_cases.rationale: Page feature-detects the API and shows both the supported and unsupported branches (try/catch + missing-API messaging).

references.rationale: Page links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6024844719947776 directly in the see-also / references section.

design_system.rationale: All colours flow through CSS variables, no raw hex, and button overrides keep colour and background in sync.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface
openQuestions.detail: The page has the bones of an interactive demo but only 1 controls. Add another axis the user can vary (a second parameter, a different input mode, or an explicit before/after toggle) and route the result into a visible readout.

openQuestions.title: Tighten lede / blurb vocabulary alignment
openQuestions.detail: Lede on the concept and blurb on the feature index share only one or two anchor words. Either restate the blurb's promise in the lede, or update the blurb on /v141/webassembly-custom-descriptors/ to match what the demo actually shows.

summary: Hits the bar but several criteria sit at partial — the open questions list concrete additions that would close the gap.
